content: In continuation to the basic motor movements that we learnt in Grades 3 and 4, we use our large muscles for movements such as jumping, throwing, catching, kicking, and hitting. These are known as gross motor skills, which play a vital role in everyday tasks, sports, and maintaining physical fitness.
learning_objectives: Understand gross motor skills and their importance
Identify key motor movements: throwing, catching, kicking, receiving, and striking
Recognize the difference between gross and fine motor skills

content: Learn the fundamentals of throwing and catching through engaging games.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of focusing on the target when throwing.
Recognize how a relaxed stance aids in catching.
Appreciate the significance of a firm grip for both throwing and catching.

content: Catching a ball involves anticipating where it will land, positioning your body behind the ball, and using your hands to form a 'cup' to softly receive the ball. Your hands should be soft and your eyes focused on the ball.
learning_objectives: Anticipate the ball's landing spot
Body positioning is crucial; stay behind the ball
Form a 'cup' with your hands to catch the ball softly

topic: Bones and Agility Development in 'Hush Hush Throw'
content: The human body is structured by 206 bones that provide shape and support; the game 'Hush Hush Throw' helps develop agility and coordination.
learning_objectives: Bones give the human body structure and shape.
Agility and coordination are developed through quick decision-making skills.
Varying the game's difficulty can enhance skill development.

content: When deciding whom to throw the ball to, consider the following factors: 1. Distance; 2. Target; 3. Skill Level.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of distance in determining the type of throw.
Recognize the significance of accuracy in throwing.
Acknowledge how skill level affects the complexity of the throw.

topic: Motor Skills Development Through Ball Throwing
content: Practice basic motor movements by throwing a ball against a wall and catching it to develop coordination, concentration, and reaction time.
learning_objectives: Vary the size of the ball to challenge different motor skills.
Vary the count and number of repetitions to improve endurance and precision.
Use different throwing skills to enhance adaptability and technique.

content: Develop agility, coordination, and teamwork.
learning_objectives: Pass the ball to the correct teammate to score a point.
Intercept the ball and pass it thrice before throwing it to the other half.
Determine the winning team based on the maximum points at the end of the game.

content: In this activity, students will learn to work together to score points by successfully receiving a ball in designated areas.
learning_objectives: Understand that successful receptions in the larger box earn one point
Understand that successful receptions in the smaller box earn two points
Recognize that the team with the most points at the end of the game wins

content: Develop accuracy in throwing and reaction time by varying the size, weight, and shape of the ball.
learning_objectives: Accuracy in throwing improves with practice.
Reaction time is crucial for successful catching.
Varying ball characteristics helps develop adaptability.

topic: Khel Yoga: Grade 5 Bull s Eye
content: Form teams of 4-5 students, place three markers (Spot A, B, C), run to Spot B, pick up ball, throw at target, run back to Spot B, repeat.
learning_objectives: Develop power in throws
Foster quick responses
Improve precision and accuracy

content: Kicking involves using our feet to direct the ball towards a target, while receiving is about controlling the ball when it comes to us.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of a relaxed stance in kicking
Recognize the role of a calculated approach in achieving accurate kicks
Appreciate the significance of a smooth follow-through in kicking
Learn how to control the ball with feet to maintain possession during receiving
Realize that both kicking and receiving are essential for effective and enjoyable football

content: Keep the non-kicking leg to the side and use the inner or top part of the kicking foot. Swing your arms sideways and follow through in the direction of the kick.
learning_objectives: Keep the non-kicking leg to the side for stability.
Use the inner or top part of the kicking foot for control and power.
Follow through with the arms to maintain balance and add momentum.

content: To develop accuracy in kicking a football, students must learn to balance their body as the ball touches the ground and attempt to dribble it after receiving.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of balancing the body when receiving the ball
Learn to position the kicking leg correctly
Practice juggling the ball to improve kicking skills

content: Ensure safety while playing a game that involves kicking a ball.
learning_objectives: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart to maintain balance.
Keep your eyes on the ball and your body between the ball and the opponent.
Use your legs to kick the ball, not your hands or arms.

content: Form teams of 5 students each. Provide one football and a cone to each team. The student will place the cone at a distance of 5 feet from the wall and place the ball next to the cone. The aim is to kick the ball onto the wall in a way that it will rebound and hit the cone.
learning_objectives: Understand that elasticity allows a ball to return to its shape after impact.
Recognize that momentum carries the ball forward after the bounce.
Realize that the combination of elasticity and momentum results in the ball bouncing.

content: Developing a sense of force and work with angles while kicking.
learning_objectives: Understand how varying the number of students in teams affects gameplay.
Recognize the impact of using different objects as hitting targets.
Appreciate the significance of rebound angles in kicking.
Observe how force applied influences the ball's trajectory and distance.
Acknowledge the role of angles in controlling the ball's direction and efficiency.
Practice kicking with different forces and observe changes in the ball's path.

content: In this activity, students will learn about the importance of teamwork and coordination in sports. They will also understand how varying the number of players and the size of the playing area can affect the game.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of teamwork and coordination in sports
Recognize how varying team size affects game dynamics
Develop leg-eye coordination, sense of space, and sense of force with respect to distance

topic: Adjusting Movements to Hit a Moving Target
content: In this activity, students will learn to adjust their movements to hit a moving target.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of adjusting speed and direction when hitting a moving object.
Develop spatial awareness and timing to successfully hit the target.
Work in teams to improve coordination and communication.

topic: Striking an Object with a Racquet
content: Proper stance is crucial for balance and readiness to hit the object. The grip on the racquet should be firm with the top hand and more relaxed with the bottom hand to control direction. The hitting motion involves stepping forward, rotating the hips and shoulders, keeping eyes on the object, and extending arms fully at the point of contact.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of a proper stance for balance and readiness.
Learn how to maintain a correct grip to control the direction of the hit.
Master the components of the hitting motion including stepping, rotating, eye focus, and arm extension.

content: Swings the upper body along with the racquet, which goes around the body, and finishes over the shoulder. Anticipate the object and move towards it. Reach and swing the racquet. Rotate shoulders and hips. After hitting, follow through as shown in the picture. Stand with knees slightly bent and the racquet facing the direction of the incoming object. Bend forward and hold the racquet in both hands. Keep the hands gentle on impact.
learning_objectives: The follow-through is essential for accuracy and power in racquet sports.
Anticipation and proper stance are crucial for effective movement and reaction.
The grip and receiving technique ensure control and safety.

content: Vary the distance between the start and finish lines. Vary the actions, such as balancing multiple balls, etc. Vary the tapping with forehand, backhand, and mixed.
learning_objectives: To improve adaptability and reaction times by varying distances and actions
To enhance concentration and coordination by balancing multiple balls
To develop versatility in playing techniques by practicing different strokes

topic: Developing Coupling Movements Through Team Balloon Hitting
content: students will learn to develop coupling movements by hitting a balloon while running, which requires cooperation among teammates
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of cooperation among teammates
Recognize how varying the size of the playing area affects the game dynamics
Identify the benefits of using soft balls instead of balloons

topic: Striking vs Hitting in a Game
content: Striking is when you hit the ball with a bat, and hitting is when you throw the ball high, straight, or low.
learning_objectives: Striking requires hand-eye coordination and accuracy.
Hitting adds variations to the game by changing the ball's trajectory.
Both actions are essential for developing motor skills.

content: Playing outdoor games like Khel Yoga and Corner Tennis helps improve motor skills, balance, and coordination.
learning_objectives: Outdoor games enhance physical abilities.
Motor skills, balance, and coordination are crucial for overall physical development.
Quick decision-making and anticipation are developed through these games.

content: Divide the students into equal teams and mark a square of 10 10 meters with three bases and a smaller square at one corner.
learning_objectives: Understand the objective of the game
Recognize the importance of body positioning and angle in hitting the ball
Identify the roles of the batting and fielding teams

topic: Developing Upper Body Strength, Speed, and Hand-Eye Coordination
content: Varying the size of the balls and the field enhances the challenge and engages different muscle groups; discussing other upper body strengthening activities can provide a comprehensive approach to physical development; the game of 'Hidden Ball' with a stick and a ball (tennis or soft/cushion balls) promotes coordination and accuracy
learning_objectives: Understand how varying ball sizes and fields challenges the body and promotes muscle growth
Identify other upper body strengthening activities to complement physical development
Recognize the importance of hand-eye coordination in motor movements

content: Adjusting position while retrieving the ball hit by teammates. Setting and achieving individual goals for balance and strength.
learning_objectives: Adjust your position quickly when the ball is hit towards you.
Set realistic individual goals for balance and strength.
Understand the importance of varying the size of the circle.

content: Developing coordination and balance, enhancing motor skills through alternating limb movements, improving agility by changing directions
learning_objectives: To understand the importance of alternating limb movements in developing coordination and balance
To recognize how changing directions during the activity benefits a student's motor skills
To appreciate why coordination is crucial for effective physical activity

content: To develop speed and lower body strength, students will touch the cone at the finish line, run back to the starting line, and clap the next student's hand, repeating until the last student runs.
learning_objectives: To understand the purpose of the human body's rapid response system.
To recognize how understanding this concept improves physical performance.
To identify a sport where rapid response is important.

content: Develop upper body and lower body strength, vary the number of repetitions, proper rest and sleep are crucial for performance
learning_objectives: To understand the importance of varying repetitions in physical activities
To recognize the impact of rest and sleep on physical performance
To develop both upper and lower body strength through varied exercises

content: Develop lower body strength and a sense of accomplishment through varied jumping exercises.
learning_objectives: Jog for 7-10 minutes before the activity to warm up muscles.
Perform different types of jumps and use varied cones to improve motor skills.
Measure the distance of the jump and set a higher target for the next attempt to enhance performance.

content: The human body prioritizes certain stimuli over others; loud noises are often processed faster by the auditory system; visual stimuli like flashing lights may require more cognitive processing time.
learning_objectives: Understand why loud noises cause quicker reactions than flashing lights
Identify the part of the body responsible for processing sounds
Describe a situation where a flashing light might be more effective than a loud noise

content: Discuss and create similar activities, play and have fun. Reprint 2026-27 66 Khel Yoga| Grade 5 Guided self-assessment for Students The purpose of this exercise is to track the children's progress in Physical Education classes, emphasizing self-improvement and personal growth rather than comparison with others. In Grade 5, the emphasis is on developing basic motor movements, developing specific personal and social behavior, getting mentally engaged in activities (Bhartiya Khel), and getting introduced to self-assessment. This ensures a holistic and supportive learning environment.
learning_objectives: Track progress in Physical Education classes
Emphasize self-improvement and personal growth
Develop basic motor movements
Develop specific personal and social behavior
Get mentally engaged in activities
Get introduced to self-assessment

topic: Catching a Ball with One Hand
content: The ability to catch a ball with one hand requires consistent practice to improve.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of consistency in catching a ball with one hand.
Recognize how a stable stance contributes to a successful catch.
Acknowledge the role of appreciation of peers in fostering a supportive learning environment.

content: Use proper technique to kick the ball. Adjust your power based on the distance. Improve accuracy and direction for better control.
learning_objectives: Practice kicking the ball with different levels of power and observe the distance it travels.
Work on your technique by kicking the ball with proper form and control.
Adjust your kicking power for different distances.

content: Swing back the kicking leg and bring it forward to kick the ball, then move the opposite arm forward and balance the body. Shift weight onto the non-receiving foot, receive the ball with the instep while moving the foot slightly back on contact, then stay low and balance by adjusting body weight as needed.
learning_objectives: Understand the technique of kicking a ball.
Learn how to receive a ball using the instep.
Identify common mistakes in hitting the ball.

topic: Proper Technique for Hitting a Ball in Sports
content: Students will learn the proper technique for hitting a ball in sports, which includes standing sideways with legs shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and eyes on the ball.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of a proper stance for effective ball hitting.
Recognize the role of hip and shoulder rotation in generating power during the swing.
Appreciate the significance of follow-through in achieving power and accuracy.

content: Regular physical activity strengthens the body and improves overall health, overcoming physical challenges builds resilience and mental strength, many games require cooperation and communication among players, playing games connects players with their heritage and promotes holistic development, participating in a local or traditional game and reflecting on the experience, creating a safety guideline for a game you play
learning_objectives: Recognize the importance of physical activity for personal development
Understand how physical activity improves fitness and mental toughness
Appreciate the role of teamwork in games
Connect traditional games with cultural heritage
Develop safety guidelines for games

content: A game played by two teams of 7-8 students where one team throws a ball using different techniques and the other team kicks it beyond the opponent's finish line.
learning_objectives: To develop hand-eye coordination
To develop leg-eye coordination
To understand the rules of the game

content: In this game, we focus on developing strength and teamwork. The game involves forming two teams with seven students each. One team stands inside a circle, while the other team stands outside. The objective is for the outside team to push the inside team's students out of the circle within 30 seconds.
learning_objectives: Develop arm and leg strength for pushing
Vary the size of the circle to change game difficulty
Promote teamwork and strategic thinking

content: Students are divided into groups based on the number called by the game leader who stands inside a circle and randomly announces a number between 1 and 9. Leftover students who cannot form a full group are eliminated.
learning_objectives: Understand grouping based on announced numbers
Recognize the game leader's role in announcing numbers and standing inside the circle
Understand the elimination process for students who cannot form a full group

topic: Teamwork and Decision-Making Through a Group Game
content: In this game, students are divided into groups and compete in a final round to win. The last student remaining in the circle after each round is the winner.
learning_objectives: Promotes teamwork and quick decision-making
Teaches about inclusion and dealing with feelings of being left out
Relates to real-life scenarios where teamwork and quick thinking are essential

topic: Concept Block 42 - Attacker and Defender Game
content: Players take turns being either an attacker or a defender, where the attacker tries to tag the defender who must perform various actions to avoid being tagged.
learning_objectives: Understand the roles of attacker and defender in the game.
Identify the actions a defender can perform to avoid being tagged.
Learn how a defender switches to another defender.
Recognize what happens when a student becomes the attacker.
Observe and imitate the attacker's movements closely.
Play the game with a partner, taking turns being the attacker and defender.
Create a new boundary for the game, such as a square or zig-zag pattern.
Avoid not performing at least three different actions before saying 'Kho!'
Avoid not switching to another defender when tagged.

content: In this game, non-verbal cues such as body language and movement patterns are crucial for both attackers and defenders to understand each other's intentions and strategies.
learning_objectives: Non-verbal cues are essential for communication during the game.
The game requires balance, coordination, agility, and quick reflexes.
Starting with multiple attackers makes the game more challenging.

content: Frogs can only move by hopping, making it harder but funnier! The Damru Relay is a game that tests students' agility and teamwork.
learning_objectives: Frogs move by hopping.
Hopping is a unique form of movement for frogs.
Hopping is adapted to a frog's environment.
Agility involves covering distance efficiently.
Teams compete to complete laps around a track.
Running backwards can improve agility.
Safety is paramount in all physical activities.
No kicking or harmful contact is allowed.
Gentle touches are allowed for tagging.

content: Divide students into two teams and have them face each other at a distance of 15 to 20 meters. The defenders stand with both arms extended, one hand at head level and one hand at waist level, palms facing each other. The attackers will take turns attempting to clap the defenders' palms and return safely.
learning_objectives: Understand the rules of Kabaddi
Recognize the roles of attackers and defenders
Explain the importance of chanting 'Kabaddi, Kabaddi'

content: Participants form circles with 7-8 students each, spaced 3-4 feet apart. The 'denner' starts as a lion, roaring and moving around the field.
learning_objectives: Agility, reaction time, strategic thinking, and breath control are improved through gameplay.
Teamwork and excitement are promoted.
Sportsmanship is essential for a positive gaming experience.

content: In this game, students are divided into groups called circles. Each group has a leader known as the denner. The denner's role is to call out a wild animal's name, which serves as a signal for students to chase the animal, aiming to tap its back. The student who successfully taps the animal's back is named Veer and becomes the new denner.
learning_objectives: Develop core strength and anticipation among students
Simulate a jungle's food chain with the denner representing the predator and students representing various animals
Understand the dynamics of the food chain and the importance of each organism's role

content: Students develop anticipation, perception, spatial awareness, and a sense of patriotism for their homeland by participating in a game where they must find a hidden object (soil) on a field.
learning_objectives: Use senses and spatial awareness to locate the hidden object
Foster teamwork and cooperation among students
Develop a sense of responsibility and discipline

content: To develop core strength, variations in exercises like sit-ups can be beneficial for different muscle groups, understanding directions is crucial in activities like soil finding, and core muscles are essential for various body movements and stability.
learning_objectives: Explain the importance of core muscles for body movements and stability
Discuss the benefits of exercise variations like sit-ups
Highlight the significance of understanding directions in activities

topic: Spatial Awareness and Agility Through Game Play
content: In the game described, students must navigate a house while avoiding being touched by students outside the house.
learning_objectives: Develop spatial awareness and agility.
Implement strategies such as quick changes in direction, using corners, and creating distractions.
Understand the impact of modifying game variables on difficulty.
Recognize the importance of spatial awareness in real-life scenarios.
Practice and refine avoidance techniques through gameplay.
Identify common mistakes like overconfidence and lack of attention.
Revise and enhance understanding through practical application.

content: Form two circles where one thrower throws a ball into an inner circle while students inside try to stop it with one leg; if successful, they become the new thrower.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of eating healthy foods like lentils and pulses.
Recognize that lentils and pulses are good sources of protein and nutrients.
Appreciate how a balanced diet contributes to overall health.

topic: Monitoring Social and Emotional Development Through Physical Education
content: The purpose of this exercise is to assist parents and guardians in monitoring their child's social and emotional development through Physical Education.
learning_objectives: Identify the focus of the exercise
Discuss various positions for ball hitting
Rate enjoyability and participation in physical activities

content: Helping others and kindness are essential aspects of a positive community involving empathy, respect, and a willingness to support and include others.
learning_objectives: Regularly offering help without being asked demonstrates a proactive approach to kindness.
Engaging in helping others with genuine empathy shows a deep understanding of their needs.
Caring for others and helping whenever needed fosters a supportive environment.
Helping others and kindness create a cycle of positivity by encouraging recipients to pay it forward.
Assuming others want help without asking first is a common mistake.
Helping others with the wrong intentions, such as seeking personal gain, is a common mistake.
Empathy is crucial for understanding and supporting others.
Setting a positive example contributes to a supportive community.

topic: Taking Responsibility for Actions and Duties
content: This section teaches Grade 5 students about taking responsibility for their actions and duties, emphasizing the importance of initiative, planning, and completing tasks on time.
learning_objectives: Take initiative in handling schoolwork and daily duties without needing frequent reminders
Regularly take responsibility for personal and shared duties
Plan tasks effectively and finish them on time, demonstrating a balance between different duties

content: Yoga is a holistic practice that promotes physical health and mental peace through asanas, pranayama, and meditation.
learning_objectives: Yoga asanas contribute to physical strength and flexibility.
Pranayama and Dhyana are essential for mental well-being.
Yamas, Niyamas, and Pratyahara are foundational to ethical living.

content: Yoga is a practice that combines physical postures, breathing techniques, and meditation to promote health and well-being.
learning_objectives: Yoga helps in maintaining physical health by improving flexibility, strength, and balance.
It supports mental health by reducing stress and anxiety through mindfulness and meditation.
Yoga fosters a sense of community and connection when practiced in groups.

content: Yoga is a holistic practice that encompasses physical postures (asanas), breathing techniques (pranayama), and meditation.
learning_objectives: Identify the three main components of yoga.
Explain how asanas benefit physical health.
Describe the purpose of pranayama in yoga.

content: When we do anything with joy in our hearts and calmness in our minds, it becomes yoga. Every action done with awareness, happiness, and dedication is yoga.
learning_objectives: Understand that yoga is a holistic practice encompassing physical, mental, and spiritual well-being
Recognize the importance of awareness, happiness, and dedication in performing actions as yoga
Appreciate the significance of joy and calmness in practicing yoga

content: In this class, we learn about three important paths of yoga: Karma Yoga (the path of action), Gyana Yoga (the path of knowledge), and Bhakti Yoga (the path of devotion).
learning_objectives: Understand Karma Yoga as selflessly performing actions for the benefit of others.
Recognize Gyana Yoga as focusing on gaining knowledge and wisdom.
Identify Bhakti Yoga as expressing love and devotion towards a higher power or the divine.

content: Karma Yoga is a spiritual practice that focuses on selfless service. It teaches us to perform our duties and help others without expecting anything in return.
learning_objectives: Understand that Karma Yoga involves performing actions with love and without attachment to the results.
Recognize how Karma Yoga helps in purifying the heart and mind by serving others.
Identify that Karma Yoga aids in developing compassion and empathy towards all beings.

content: Karma yoga is the practice of performing our duties with honesty, love, and effort, without expecting any rewards. Karma yoga teaches us to act selflessly and with integrity. It emphasizes the importance of doing our work with a positive attitude. The practice of karma yoga helps us to focus on the present moment and our actions.
learning_objectives: Understand what karma yoga is
Identify the key differences between karma yoga and other forms of yoga
List the benefits of practicing karma yoga

content: Karma Yoga is a spiritual practice that focuses on selfless service to others without seeking personal gain or recognition.
learning_objectives: Understand that Karma Yogis perform their duties with the sole intention of benefiting society, not for fame or appreciation.
Recognize that Karma Yoga teaches individuals to act with a sense of duty and responsibility towards others.
Comprehend that the joy in Karma Yoga comes from the act of service itself, rather than in the outcomes or rewards.

topic: Concept Block 63 - The Core Concept of Gyana Yoga
content: The core concept revolves around the idea that the answers to life's big questions are not found in material possessions or external sources, but within oneself through the practice of Gyana Yoga.
learning_objectives: Understand that happiness is not derived from material possessions
Recognize the importance of individuality and the universe's vastness
Define Gyana Yoga as a path to self-discovery and finding answers within oneself

content: Gyana Yoga is a spiritual practice that involves reading books, asking questions, thinking deeply, and learning from our daily experiences.
learning_objectives: The main goal of Gyana Yoga is to gain knowledge and understanding.
Gyana Yoga helps us by encouraging curiosity and reflection in our daily lives.
Gyana Yoga leads to personal and spiritual growth through the process of learning and self-improvement.

content: Swami Chinmayananda, a revered Gyana yogi, established numerous centers globally and contributed to the construction of schools, hospitals, nursing homes, and clinics.
learning_objectives: Identify Swami Chinmayananda's global influence through the establishment of centers and institutions.
Understand his role as a Gyana yogi and his contributions to the Arya Samaj movement.
Discuss the importance of reading and learning from various sources, including literature and autobiographies.

content: Yoga and Vedanta are spiritual practices that originated in India, emphasizing personal growth, ethical living, and the pursuit of knowledge.
learning_objectives: Understand the core principles of yoga and Vedanta
Recognize how these practices promote personal growth and ethical living
Identify the importance of a curious and inquisitive mindset for the pursuit of knowledge

content: Bhakti Yoga is a spiritual practice that involves loving God with a pure heart and recognizing the divine presence in all aspects of life.
learning_objectives: Understand that Bhakti Yoga is about expressing love and devotion towards God.
Recognize that Bhakti Yoga encourages seeing God in everyone and everything around us.
Comprehend that practicing Bhakti Yoga can lead to miracles.

content: The concept of bhakti yoga involves practicing love and devotion towards a personal deity. This spiritual path emphasizes the importance of emotional connection and service to the divine.
learning_objectives: Understand that bhakti yoga is a spiritual practice focusing on devotion and love towards a personal deity
Identify key practices of bhakti yoga such as prayer, singing bhajans, and living a life of service
Recognize notable figures associated with bhakti yoga including Sant Kabir, Tulsidas, Akkamahadevi, and Ramakrishna Paramahamsa

topic: Asteya in Ashtanga Yoga and Beyond
content: Asteya is the principle of non-stealing, which applies to actions, words, and thoughts. It emphasizes not taking what does not belong to us, including not claiming credit for others' work.
learning_objectives: Asteya promotes honesty and integrity in all aspects of life.
It extends beyond physical possessions to include intellectual property and personal achievements.
Asteya fosters a respectful and ethical community.

content: Kavya finds Adya's butterfly hair clip and faces a decision between keeping it and returning it.
learning_objectives: Understand the conflict between personal desire and moral responsibility
Recognize the importance of honesty and integrity
Learn the benefits of making difficult ethical decisions

content: Returning lost items is a moral action that reflects honesty and respect for others' property.
learning_objectives: Returning lost items helps maintain trust within a community.
Keeping lost items can lead to guilt and negative feelings.
Acting with integrity can earn respect from others.

content: The core concept revolves around the moral dilemma faced by a character named Kavya, who finds a hair clip and decides to keep it, despite knowing it's wrong. She struggles with guilt and eventually confesses her mistake to her teacher.
learning_objectives: Honesty is a fundamental value.
Guilt can be a powerful motivator for change.
Confronting our mistakes is essential for personal growth.

content: In situations where we face temptation, it's important to resist by remembering the consequences of our actions and the value of honesty.
learning_objectives: Recognize the temptation and the potential consequences of giving in.
Consider the feelings of others and the impact of our actions on them.
Seek support from trusted individuals when facing difficult choices.

topic: Self-discipline and dedication in Ashtanga yoga
content: Self-discipline and dedication are essential for personal development, part of Ashtanga yoga's Niyama, emphasizing self-control, purity, and commitment.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of self-discipline in achieving personal growth
Recognize the importance of purity for a healthy lifestyle
Appreciate the significance of dedication in goal achievement

topic: The Importance of Establishing Good Habits for Personal Growth
content: Good habits, such as waking up early, practicing yoga, and avoiding gadgets, contribute to a disciplined and healthy lifestyle; reflecting on one's actions and feelings at the end of the week helps in self-improvement; learning from the lives of role models can inspire and guide personal development
learning_objectives: Understand the benefits of establishing good habits
Recognize the importance of self-reflection for personal growth
Identify role models and understand their impact on personal development

content: Pratyahara is the fifth limb of Ashtanga Yoga, which focuses on the withdrawal of the senses to achieve a state of inner peace and concentration.
learning_objectives: Pratyahara involves turning the attention inward and away from external stimuli.
It is a preparatory step for meditation and higher practices in yoga.
Pratyahara helps in developing self-control and awareness.

content: King Dasharatha's hunting technique relied on his ability to use sound to locate prey, Swami Vivekananda's simple, light meals contributed to his physical and mental well-being, Regular yoga practice and a balanced lifestyle can lead to better health and focus, Eating junk food can lead to feeling dull and sleepy, while fruits and healthy food can make one feel energetic and active, Raju's regular yoga and study routine keeps him healthy and energetic, whereas Ramu's lack of routine and distractions lead to illness and difficulty in completing homework
learning_objectives: To understand the importance of healthy eating
To recognize the benefits of regular yoga practice
To identify the consequences of an unbalanced lifestyle

content: Pratyahara is the practice of controlling our senses and directing them with purpose, which supports the cultivation of effort, focus, alertness, discipline, and wise choices.
learning_objectives: Understand the core concept of Pratyahara
Identify the five key qualities supported by Pratyahara
Recognize the importance of Pratyahara in modern yoga asana classes
Describe how Pratyahara enhances focus and concentration during yoga practice
Explain why neglecting Pratyahara can lead to a lack of focus
Acknowledge that Pratyahara is beneficial for all levels of yoga practitioners

topic: Padanguli Naman and Related Yoga Exercises
content: Padanguli Naman is a yoga exercise that focuses on the flexibility and movement of the toes, improving the flexibility of the toes and ankles, aiding in maintaining the balance and stability of the feet, and is beneficial for those who spend long hours sitting or standing.
learning_objectives: Understand the benefits of Padanguli Naman
Explain how Padanguli Naman improves the flexibility of the toes and ankles
Identify situations where Padanguli Naman can be particularly beneficial
Perform Padanguli Naman and Gulpha Chakra Chalana and Gulpha Naman exercises correctly
Avoid common mistakes during the exercises
Revise the key points of the exercises

topic: Yoga Asanas for Hip Mobility and Balance
content: This section explores yoga asanas that focus on hip mobility and balance, including the butterfly pose (Baddha Konasana), hip rotation exercises, and balancing exercises.
learning_objectives: Understand the benefits of the butterfly pose (Baddha Konasana) for opening the hips and improving circulation.
Recognize the importance of hip rotation exercises for maintaining the health of the hip joints.
Appreciate the role of balancing exercises in developing proprioception and coordination.

content: Dynamic spinal twists and churning the mill are yoga exercises.
learning_objectives: Understand the steps of Gatyatmak Meru Vakra Sthiti (Dynamic spinal twist).
Learn how to perform Chakki Chalana (Churning the mill).
Practice alternating sides for 10 rounds in Dynamic spinal twists.

content: While bending backwards, inhale and keep the elbows straight. Practice for 10 rounds in the clockwise direction. Then repeat for 10 rounds in the anti-clockwise direction. Feel the stretch in the hands, legs, lower back, and abdomen. Sit straight with your legs extended in front and hands by the side of the body. Exhale, twist the trunk towards the left side and place both palms bent on the floor. Bend the arms and lower the forehead so that it touches the floor between the hands. Inhale and slowly return to the centre. Repeat the same by twisting the trunk to the right side. Lie down on your back with the legs together and arms by the side of your body. Raise both legs and make cycling movements, as if pedalling a bicycle.
learning_objectives: Practice spinal twists and cycling movements.
Feel the stretch in various parts of the body during yoga exercises.
Perform yoga exercises in a sequence.

content: Perform 10 forward rotations and 10 backwards rotations. Breathe normally throughout. After completing, slowly lower the legs and relax in Shavasana. Stand straight with your feet shoulder-width apart, extend both arms forward, exhale, slowly bend your knees, lower the body until the thighs are parallel to the ground, inhale and return to the standing position, repeat for 10 rounds.
learning_objectives: Demonstrate correct execution of forward and backward rotations
Maintain normal breathing during yoga asanas
Perform Jangha Shakti Vikasaka asana accurately
Exhibit proper form in standing and bending positions

content: Asanas are yoga postures that are practiced to develop a steady posture, maintain health, and achieve lightness of the body.
learning_objectives: Asanas improve physical fitness, flexibility, and balance.
Asanas help maintain a healthy body weight.
Asanas enhance the quality of sleep and boost immunity.
Asanas increase self-confidence.

topic: Yoga Asana Practice for Children with Medical Conditions
content: Children with specific medical conditions such as epilepsy, asthma or bronchitis, should inform the teacher before beginning practice. Hasta Uttanasana involves lifting both arms above the head, bending the trunk and head backwards, and then straightening the back and lowering the arms.
learning_objectives: Identify the importance of informing teachers about medical conditions before yoga practice.
Describe the initial position and steps of Hasta Uttanasana.
List the benefits of practicing Hasta Uttanasana.

content: Utkatasana strengthens the thigh muscles and toes, improves body balance and stability, and is named due to its demanding nature.
learning_objectives: Identify the primary muscles engaged during Utkatasana.
Explain how Utkatasana improves body balance.
Describe why maintaining a straight back is important in Utkatasana.

content: Inhale, spread legs 2 to 3 feet apart, raise hands parallel to ground, exhale, turn right foot 90 outward, left foot 60 inward, bend right knee, raise arms, join palms above head, look at them, inhale, come back to center, straighten knees, exhale, drop hands, bring legs closer, relax.
learning_objectives: This asana strengthens the lower back, arms and legs.
It also improves balance.

content: Trikonasana, also known as the Triangle Pose, is a yoga asana that forms three angles with the body.
learning_objectives: Trikonasana strengthens the legs, abdomen, and back muscles.
It stimulates the digestive organs and helps relieve constipation.
The pose involves a twist in the trunk, creating three angles with the body.

content: Parvatasana, also known as the Mountain Pose, is a yoga asana that resembles the shape of a mountain.
learning_objectives: Improves blood circulation, especially to the spine and head.
Helps to manage anger and calms the mind.
Engages the core and encourages effective blood flow distribution.
Final position resembles an inverted V shape.
Inhaling and exhaling movements help pump blood to the head and spine.
Not keeping the heels on the floor is a common mistake.
Not fully extending the arms and legs in the final position is another common mistake.

content: A seated yoga posture that stretches the hamstrings, groins, and spine, and helps to improve digestion and relieve mild depression.
learning_objectives: Perform preparatory exercises before practising Janu Sirsasana.
Understand the benefits of Janu Sirsasana.
Identify common mistakes to avoid when practicing Janu Sirsasana.

content: Stretch both legs forward and relax in Shithila Dandasana; bend the knees and place them one above the other in Gomukhasana
learning_objectives: Shithila Dandasana stimulates abdominal and pelvic organs, improving digestion
Shithila Dandasana reduces abdominal fat
Gomukhasana's cow's face shape is achieved by positioning the knees one above the other
Preparatory exercises are essential before practising Shithila Dandasana

content: Gomukhasana, also known as Cow Face Posture, is a seated yoga pose that aims to stretch and strengthen the upper body, particularly the arms, chest, and shoulders.
learning_objectives: Gomukhasana helps in expanding the chest area, which can increase lung capacity.
This asana relieves leg cramps and reduces foot stiffness.
It is named after the Hatha Yogi Rishi Matsyendranath and is beneficial for practitioners of all levels.

content: It improves the flexibility of the spine. It strengthens abdominal and back muscles. It enhances sleep quality and helps regulate digestion.
learning_objectives: Understand the benefits of Ardha Matsyendrasana
Identify the effects of the twist on the spine
List the organs stimulated by the twist

content: Inhale and lift the chest and head upwards while stretching the arms fully to pull the shoulders back. Maintain this posture for a few seconds.
learning_objectives: Sarpasana expands the lungs, facilitating better inhalation and exhalation.
It helps in relieving back aches.
It strengthens abdominal organs.

content: A yoga asana that strengthens abdominal organs and helps relieve back pain resembling a fish in its final posture
learning_objectives: Understand the benefits of Tiryaka Bhujangasana
Identify preparatory exercises for Tiryaka Bhujangasana
Explain how Tiryaka Bhujangasana helps in relieving back pain

content: This section explores a yoga asana involving a backward bend supported by forearms and elbows to enhance deep breathing and regulate thyroid gland function.
learning_objectives: Understand the preparatory steps for the asana
Identify the benefits of practicing the asana
Describe how the asana enhances deep breathing

content: This section explains the importance of maintaining a specific posture during a yoga practice. It emphasizes the need for a gap between the knees, proper alignment of the arms, and the correct position of the head and eyes.
learning_objectives: Maintain a 2-foot gap between the knees to ensure proper alignment and balance.
Keep the arms straight and the back arched to support the body weight on the arms.
Tilt the head back and gaze at the eyebrow center to maintain focus and balance.

topic: Relaxation Techniques in Khel Yoga for Grade 5
content: Lie down on the stomach, place the right arm under the head, bend the left leg sideways, interlock the fingers of both hands, rest the left elbow on the bent left knee, keep the right leg straight and relaxed, breathe normally and remain in this position, after some time change the side and repeat the steps
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of relaxation techniques
Learn the steps of performing Shavasana
Learn the steps of performing Matsya Kridasana

content: Slow and deep breathing calms the nervous system, focused breathing reduces stress and anxiety, regular practice increases lung capacity, the practice of slow and deep breathing activates the parasympathetic nervous system, Anuloma Viloma Pranayama helps balance the body's energy channels, the exercise involves breathing through a straw for five minutes with focus on slow exhalation, the exercise also includes performing Anuloma Viloma Pranayama for 10 minutes by alternating nostrils, forgetting to focus on slow exhalation during straw breathing, not alternating nostrils correctly during Anuloma Viloma Pranayama
learning_objectives: Understand how slow breathing affects the nervous system
Identify the benefits of practicing Pranayama regularly
Describe the Anuloma Viloma Pranayama technique

content: Breath awareness is a fundamental aspect of yoga that focuses on controlling and observing the breath to enhance relaxation and concentration.
learning_objectives: Breath awareness helps to calm the mind and reduce stress.
It improves lung capacity and oxygenation of the body.
It aids in maintaining a balanced state of mind and body.
Anuloma Viloma Pranayama balances the two hemispheres of the brain, leading to improved concentration and mental clarity.
Ujjayi Pranayama helps to regulate the breath and calm the mind.
Not keeping the eyes closed during practice can distract from the focus on breath.
Rushing through the breath can lead to shallow breathing and less benefit from the practice.

content: Sitkari and Sheetali Pranayamas instructions for practice.
learning_objectives: Learn to perform Sitkari Pranayama
Learn to perform Sheetali Pranayama
Understand the benefits of these pranayamas

content: Prana Mudra is a hand gesture that is believed to enhance the flow of energy (prana) in the body, leading to increased vitality and a calmer mind.
learning_objectives: Describe the steps to perform Prana Mudra
Identify the duration for holding the Prana Mudra posture
List the expected benefits of practicing Prana Mudra

content: Apana Mudra involves touching the tips of the middle and ring finger to the thumb while keeping the index and little finger straight, and Prithvi Mudra involves touching the tip of the ring finger to the tip of the thumb with the remaining fingers open.
learning_objectives: Apana Mudra improves digestion and alleviates abdominal problems.
Prithvi Mudra increases energy levels and enhances focus, memory, and concentration.
Both mudras should be practiced for 5 minutes daily.

content: Practise the Prithvi Mudra every day for 5 minutes. Maintain a journal to note any changes observed after practising.
learning_objectives: Understand the benefits of Prithvi Mudra
Learn the steps to perform Prithvi Mudra
Recognize the importance of daily practice

content: Palming is a technique used to relax the eyes and reduce eye strain by blocking out light and allowing the eyes to rest; practicing Bindu Trataka, a form of meditation, can enhance concentration and mental clarity; Om Dhyana, or chanting Om, is a meditative practice that can induce a state of calmness and focus.
learning_objectives: Understand the purpose of palming
Describe how Bindu Trataka benefits concentration
Explain the experience of Om Dhyana

content: Krida Yoga is a playful and engaging activity included in the curriculum to promote physical activity, attention, and strategy among children.
learning_objectives: Krida Yoga encourages playfulness and variety in the class.
It promotes physical activity and attention.
The activity helps develop strategic thinking.

topic: Khel Yoga Game Rules and Strategies
content: When a Runner feels about to be caught, they must stop, lift their right leg by bending it at the knee, put their hand behind the bent knee, and touch their nose with the same hand.
learning_objectives: The Runner must stop and perform a specific action to avoid capture.
The Chaser can only resume chasing after the Runner releases the position.
The Runner can evade capture by running in any direction within the circle.

content: Students will practice their awareness, concentration, and mental quickness by responding to verbal cues with specific words.
learning_objectives: Listen carefully to the leader's cue and respond with the correct word.
Continue the game until only one student remains, who will be the winner.
Practice mental agility and quick thinking.

content: For numbers that are multiples of both 3 and 5, say 'akkad bakkad.' If a player says the wrong word or says the number instead of the correct word, they are eliminated. Delayed responses are also not considered.
learning_objectives: Identify multiples of 3 and 5 using the 'akkad bakkad' concept
Understand the rules of the game for multiples of 3 and 5
Recognize the importance of timely and accurate responses

content: Personal responsibility and community service are essential for personal growth and societal well-being.
learning_objectives: Taking care of personal belongings and maintaining a clean environment reflects responsibility.
Volunteering and performing kind acts contribute to community welfare.
Actively participating in learning and household chores demonstrates a commitment to personal and family improvement.

content: Bhakti Yoga is a spiritual practice that focuses on devotion and love towards a personal deity or the divine.
learning_objectives: Express love and respect for teachers, parents, and elders
Practice Bhakti Yoga through prayer, bhajan, or chanting
Understand the importance of expressing love and respect for loved ones

content: Yoga is a holistic practice that combines physical postures, breathing techniques, and meditation to enhance physical and mental well-being.
learning_objectives: Yoga asanas improve flexibility, strength, and balance.
Pranayama helps in controlling the breath and calming the mind.
Mudras are symbolic hand gestures that influence the flow of energy in the body.

topic: Yoga and Motor Movement Activities Planning
content: The basic motor movement activities, games, and Yoga sessions can be planned in parallel for continuous and consistent development throughout the year.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of planning motor movement activities and Yoga sessions together.
Learn the structure of a Yoga session including the opening prayer and mudras.
Recognize the benefits of Yoga for physical and mental well-being.

content: Yoga is a holistic practice that encompasses physical postures, breath control, meditation, and ethical precepts.
learning_objectives: Identify the five Yamas in Yoga.
Explain how pranayama benefits the body and mind.
Name two asanas that improve flexibility.

content: Peace is a state of harmony and tranquility that should be present everywhere.
learning_objectives: Understand the core concept of peace as a state of harmony and tranquility
Identify the benefits of peace in a community
Recognize the role of individuals in promoting peace
Describe a situation where peace was successfully promoted
Explain how peace fosters understanding and cooperation among people
Write about the importance of peace in daily life
Create a poster illustrating the concept of peace
Differentiate between peace and the absence of conflict
Acknowledge that peace can be promoted without government intervention

topic: Basic Motor Movements in Physical Education
content: Basic Motor Movements are essential for developing physical skills and coordination in students, categorized into Type 1, Type 2, and Type 3 with specific objectives and activities.
learning_objectives: To understand the importance of Basic Motor Movements in physical education.
To recognize the differences between Type 1, Type 2, and Type 3 Basic Motor Movements.
To identify the benefits of practicing Basic Motor Movements.

content: This section focuses on exercises that strengthen the back, hip, core, and leg muscles. These exercises include backward kicks, seated twists, side lifts, candle pose, and bear crawl hold.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of warm-up and cool-down exercises
Learn how backward kicks target the back and hip muscles
Identify the muscles targeted by seated twists
Understand the benefits of performing side lifts
Recognize the muscles strengthened by the candle pose
Appreciate the importance of the bear crawl hold for overall strength and endurance

topic: Warm-up and Cool-down Exercises for Class 5
content: Full Body Stretch, Ankle Rockers, Wide-legged Forward Fold, Inchworm Walkout, Glute Lift
learning_objectives: To improve flexibility
To strengthen muscles
To enhance balance

content: Sit with your feet on the floor and hands behind you. Lift your body to look like a table and hold this position. Stand against an actual or imaginary wall. Raise your arms as shown in the figure below. Move your arms up and down slowly. Get into a plank position (or on your knees). Tap your opposite shoulder with one hand. Keep switching. Move sideways with one foot behind the other. Then step out again, like a dance move. Lie down and lift your left leg. Write your family name in the air using this leg. Use your right leg for your first name. Use both legs for your full name.
learning_objectives: Develop core strength through various exercises
Improve shoulder mobility and posture
Strengthen core muscles through shoulder taps
Enhance balance and coordination with side shuffles
Engage in a fun name game to develop core muscles
